title: Great Heep
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Youmay|the fictional droid|the [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] [[The Great Heep|TV special]]}} {{FAreview}} {{Droid |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:GreatHeap-NEGTD.png]] |name=Great Heep |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]],{{PolyhedronCite|170|Star Wars Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO}} states that [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] is set 15 years before the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. [[Biitu]]{{DroidsCite|The Great Heep}} |creator= |manufacturer=Civilization of the [[Abominor]]''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' |line= |model= |class= |cost= |length= |width= |height=6.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Masculine]] [[Programmer/Legends|programming]]{{Fact}} |pronouns=He/him{{Fact}} |sensor=[[Color/Legends|Yellow]] |plating=Gray, brown and [[red]] |armament= |equipment=*[[Engine/Legends|Drive engine]] pistons *Grinder blades (in [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]]) *Heavy grasping [[Limb/Legends|arms]] *Internal furnace *[[Magnetic grapple]] *Power-drain socket (in mouth) *Smokestacks *[[Tank/Legends|Tank]] treads |affiliation=*[[Abominor]] *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] }} {{Quote|Your duty is to serve me and Biitu for the rest of your days. Escape is impossible. Any droid caught trying to leave will be used for scrap.|The Great Heep|}} The '''Great Heep''' was a large, [[Gender/Legends|masculine]]-[[Programmer/Legends|programmed]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] who operated an enormous [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] [[Fuel-ore processing plant|processing plant]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Biitu]] in the early days of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The Heep was an [[Abominor]], a member of an [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of advanced, self-constructing droids, which [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] organic life on thousands of worlds before they were driven from their [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. The Heep was powered by an internal furnace that required a near-constant supply of fuel and further recharged himself by draining power from [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]]. In [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], the Heep formed an alliance with [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Terrinald Screed/Legends|Terrinald Screed]] of the Galactic Empire to mine fuel ore on Biitu, enslaving the [[Biituian|native population]] and constructing a "[[moisture eater]]" that drained all [[Water/Legends|moisture]] from the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] to ensure optimum conditions. When the moisture eater was [[Skirmish on Biitu (15 BBY)|destroyed]] by the [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] [[Mungo Baobab]] and his droids, [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], the stored water was released in a storm that extinguished the Heep's furnace and [[Death/Legends|deactivated]] the droid. ==Biography== ===Agent of the Abominor=== By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial era]], the Great Heep was already ancient. The Heep was an agent of the [[Abominor]], an [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of advanced, self-constructing [[Droid/Legends|droids]], which [[Speciecide|exterminated]] or [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] organic life on thousands of [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] as they stripped them of their natural resources. When the Abominor fought a [[Silentium–Abominor War|war]] against the [[Silentium]], a droid species which favored order over the asymmetry and chaos of the Abominor, the [[Yuuzhan Vong|dominant organic species]] were able to regroup and drive both machine societies from their [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. At least 300 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to the [[Declaration of a New Order|formation]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Great Heep somehow ended up being shut down, with his lifeless body placed in the [[Braltzamir Museum of Antiquated Machinery]] on the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world of [[The galaxy/Legends|the known galaxy]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The Great Heep's display was eventually discovered during the early Imperial era by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Terrinald Screed/Legends|Terrinald Screed]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. Alongside a team of students from the [[Imperial Engineers Academy]], Screed was able to reactivate the Abominor.{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-2|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 2|nolive=1}} ===Imperial alliance=== {{Quote|Make way, make way for your new master, the master of Biitu, the Great Heep!|The Great Heep's [[Unidentified announcer droid|announcer droid]]|}} In [[15 BBY/Legends|15 BBY]], the discovery of rich reserves of [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world of [[Biitu]] attracted the attention of the Great Heep, along with the Empire and [[Mungo Baobab]], heir to the famed [[Baobab Merchant Fleet]].{{DB|character|mungobaobab|Baobab, Mungo}} Baobab intended to establish a [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operation that would provide the native [[Biituian]]s with a fair share of the profits. The Great Heep, however, intended to enslave the local population and drain the planet of its resources.{{CSWECite|I|362|Great Heep}} The Heep formed a partnership with Screed, and together they seized control of Biitu and established a droid-run [[Fuel-ore processing plant|mining operation]] on the planet. Central to the mining operation was a tower known as the "[[moisture eater]]," which drained all [[Water/Legends|moisture]] from Biitu's [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], providing the dry environment preferred by the Abominor and a means to cool the mining equipment, at the cost of a drought that killed Biituian [[Unidentified Biituian crop plant|crops]] and left the [[Farm/Legends|farming]]-dependent natives on the verge of [[Death/Legends|death]]. [[File:ScreedHeep.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Great Heep and Admiral Screed]] The Heep took charge of running the mines and established a [[droid harem]], where he kept a large supply of unassuming [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droids]], treating them to [[Oil bath/Legends|oil baths]] and other droid pleasantries before eventually draining their energy to recharge himself. Other captured droids were put to work in the mines, under supervision by the Heep's [[sludgegulper]] droids. Any droid that attempted to escape was used as scrap metal. Baobab was captured during the initial occupation, [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]], and forced to await the arrival of Screed. The [[Merchant/Legends|merchant's]] droids, the [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and the [[3PO-series protocol droid/Legends|3PO-series protocol droid]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] were also captured by sludgegulpers as they made their way to Biitu aboard the Baobab Merchant [[Fleet/Legends|Fleet]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] ''[[Argo Moon]]''. While R2-D2 was sent to the droid harem, C-3PO was brought before the Heep with several other captured droids. The Heep was amused to learn of C-3PO's connection to Baobab and took delight in assigning the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] to work in the mines. The mining operation soon produced the first batch of fuel ore for the Empire and the Heep met Screed as the admiral arrived to collect it and to take custody of the captive Baobab. That night, as the Heep recharged by draining energy from the astromech droid [[KT-10]], C-3PO infiltrated the droid harem and warned the other astromechs about the Heep's intentions. Hearing the commotion, the Heep used his [[magnetic grapple]] to reach inside the droid harem and grab C-3PO. As punishment for the protocol droid's defiance, the Heep ordered C-3PO to be dismantled for use as spare parts, but C-3PO's warning prompted R2-D2 to escape from the harem. The following day, the Heep was processing fuel ore when he spotted the errant astromech droid. Intending to recharge from R2-D2, the Heep caught the other droid with his [[Magnet/Legends|magnetic]] grapple, but R2-D2 disconnected the magnet's power cable and dropped to a conveyor belt below. R2-D2 escaped into the ore processing machinery, where he linked up with C-3PO, who had escaped custody before he could be dismantled. When the two droids re-emerged, the Heep was waiting and caught both in a container, which he sealed and disposed of in a [[Laser/Legends|laser]] furnace. ===Final defeat=== {{Quote|Screed, you incompetent fool!''"
"''Me? You lumbering junk heap. If you weren't so wrapped up with your droid harem, none of this would have happened.''"
"''Tiny minded Imperial twit!''"
"''It was your useless droids that let out the R2 units. You're finished!|The Great Heep and Screed exchange insults as their scheme falls apart|}} [[File:InactiveHeep-TGH.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Great Heep was kept on display following his defeat.]] C-3PO and R2-D2 escaped once more and linked up with Baobab after he broke free of his cell. Sending the Biituian [[Fidge Tadrazan]] to free the astromechs from the harem, Baobab and the droids attempted to get aboard Screed's ship with the intention of using it to destroy the moisture eater. Screed attempted to stop them, but R2-D2 got away in the ship and the Imperials' efforts to shoot it down were hampered by the arrival of Tadrazan and the liberated astromechs, who started to [[Skirmish on Biitu (15 BBY)|attack]] Screed's [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. Arriving amid the chaos, the Heep blamed the situation on Screed, who in turn accused the Great Heep of failing to keep control of his droid harem. However, their bickering came to an abrupt end when R2-D2 opened the ship's cargo doors and dropped its load of ore containers before opening fire on the moisture eater. Attempting to restore order, the Heep summoned a pair of sludgegulpers and ordered them to destroy the cargo ship. When R2-D2 returned fire and destroyed the enemy droids, the Heep picked up a large metal disc from a nearby scrap pile and threw it at the cargo ship. The disc struck the vessel, causing it to lose altitude, but R2-D2 sent it on a collision course with the tower before abandoning ship. The cargo ship collided with the tower, causing the stored moisture to be released in a violent storm. With the destruction of the moisture eater, the enraged Heep turned his attention on Baobab, C-3PO and Tadrazan, attempting to crush them beneath his heavy grasping [[Limb/Legends|arms]]. The group fled, but Tadrazan fell, and Baobab stopped to help him. As the Heep raised his massive fist to crush the pair, he was struck by a bolt of lightning which severed his right arm, causing it to land harmlessly nearby. The Heep made one last desperate attempt at revenge, but as he raised his left arm to strike, the torrential rain poured into his furnace, extinguishing it and deactivating the droid. With the Heep gone, Screed and the other Imperials were easily rounded up by the natives and forced to help them restore their destroyed crops. The Great Heep was defeated without his Imperial overseers ever learning of his true age, and his deactivation deprived [[Archaeology/Legends|archaeologists]] of a rare opportunity to learn about the Abominor. The Biituians initially kept the Heep's body on display, attaching a large bell to the pole that extended from his head in place of his magnetic grapple. Sometime prior to [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]], however, the Great Heep was found by [[Ebenn Q3 Baobab/Legends|Ebenn Q3 Baobab]], a relative of Mungo Baobab, who restored the droid and placed his inactive body on permanent display at the [[Baobab Museum of Science]].[[Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide (real-life book)|''Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide'']] In the following years, the Heep was studied by a team of researchers who concluded that as many as eighteen Abominors may have infiltrated the galaxy. The revelation led the researcher [[Professor/Legends|Professor]] [[Cantebarius H. Broom]] to develop a phobia of all droids and to set about trying to destroy any that came into his hands. ==Characteristics== {{Quote|Curse you, Baobab!|The Great Heep's final words|}} [[File:HungryHeep-TGH.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Great Heep holds R2-D2 captive on his magnetic grapple while consuming fuel ore.]] The Great Heep was a [[Class four droid/Legends|class four droid]] with a [[Gender/Legends|masculine]] personality. Like all fourth-degree droids, the Heep was built for violence. At 6.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height, he was enormous, but still small in comparison to other Abominor, some of whom grew to be as large as a planet. Abominor grew by grafting often unnecessary machinery onto their bodies, and the Heep's roughly cuboid body was composed of assorted droid parts and tubing.''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' With rust setting in, the plates comprising the Heep's [[Hull/Legends|hull]] varied in [[Color/Legends|color]], giving him a patchwork appearance composed of shades of metallic gray, brown and red. On either side of the Heep's underside were two parallel sets of [[wheel]]s used to drive [[Tank/Legends|tank]] treads, allowing the droid to easily maneuver over uneven terrain. Above each track was a drive belt, which connected to a set of visible drive engine pistons that moved up and down in rhythm. Like other Abominor, the Heep was powered by a furnace. These internal [[Fire/Legends|fires]] were vulnerable to concentrated moisture, leading Abominor to prefer dry environments, such as the one the Heep created on Biitu. The furnace produced noxious gases, which were released through seven smokestacks of varying sizes located on top and at the rear of the Heep. Many Abominor constructed faces for themselves to mock the organic slaves that served them; the Heep's own face mimicked that of an organic, with a mouth, two roughly triangular [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and appendages resembling a [[Nose/Legends|nose]] and two [[Ear/Legends|ears]]. The Heep's jaw moved on hinges, allowing him to open and close his mouth, although he did not need to do so when talking. The mouth was filled with various cables and machinery, including grinder blades and an astromech droid-shaped socket through which the Heep could drain the life energy from smaller droids to recharge himself. The lifeless shells of the Heep's victims were ejected after he absorbed all their energy. While on Biitu, the Heep also consumed fuel ore through his mouth, delivered via a large conveyor belt. Both the mouth and eyes had a faint, [[Color/Legends|yellow]] glow, which became vivid red when the Heep was angry. When draining the energy from an astromech droid, the Heep's mouth glowed bright blue, and the normally black "pupils" faded into the yellow glow of the eyes. [[File:FunnyLookingBattery-TGH.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Great Heep recharges by draining energy from an astromech droid.]] The Heep had a magnetic grapple that could be used to lift smaller droids and equipment. The grapple, a large, circular magnet, was suspended on a cable from a pole that could be retracted into the ridge on top of the Heep's head when not in use. A second cable carried power to the magnet and could be disconnected to disable the magnet. Two large gripper arms extended from either side of the Heep's body, each ending in a red, three-fingered hand with one digit opposing the other two to allow the Heep to grip objects. The hands were powerful enough to easily lift and crush smaller droids but could also be used to safely lift an organic or to manipulate objects such as door hatches. The arms were hinged and could bend in either direction. The Heep was attended by several repair droids, while other small droids lived on his massive hull like [[Parasite/Legends|parasites]]. During his work with the Empire, the Heep kept two delirious [[Human/Legends|Human]] slaves nearby to feed his massive boilers with fuel. Like other Abominor, the Great Heep desired power and slaves. Despite his disregard for organic life, he was prepared to work with the Empire to achieve his goals and even showed deference to Admiral Screed during their joint venture. However, when Baobab and his allies brought the mining operation to an end, the Heep was quick to blame Screed for its failure. The Heep consumed everything in his path and thought nothing of destroying Biitu's ecosystem to ensure his own survival. He frequently displayed amusement at the suffering he caused, both to organics and other droids, whom he was willing to sacrifice to consume their power or to use for scrap parts. When the Biitu mining operation fell apart, the Heep was determined to get revenge on Baobab, attempting to crush the Human beneath his giant arms even as the rain waters poured into his furnace. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|For the design of ''Droids'', we were lucky to have Joe Johnston on a few shows, as the designer of ''The Great Heep'' special. He did many designs which the animators then took a step further into animation.|''Droids'' associate producer and story editor [[Paul Dini]] in 1988|{{Starlog|Yearbook Vol. 3|Saturday Morning "Star Wars"}}}} [[File:GreatHeep-SWEncyclopedia.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Great Heep]] The Great Heep was created by [[Ben Burtt]] and designed by conceptual artist [[Joe Johnston]] for ''[[The Great Heep]]'', a TV special of the [[Nelvana]] animated series [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars: Droids'']] which was first broadcast on [[June 7]], [[1986]].{{InsiderCite|32|Caravan of Courage: Star Wars on Television|''Caravan of Courage'': ''Star Wars'' on Television}} The character was voiced by [[Wikipedia:Blues|blues]] performer and voice actor [[Long John Baldry]]. Burtt later referenced the character in his [[2001]] [[Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide (real-life book)|''Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide'']], establishing that the remains of the Great Heep were put on display at the Baobab Museum of Science. The Great Heep's origins were not explained until the release of [[Daniel Wallace]]'s ''[[The New Essential Guide to Droids]]'' in [[2006]]. Wallace identified the Great Heep as an Abominor and established a link between them and the Silentium,{{Blog|danwallace|53|Endnotes for The New Essential Guide to Droids: PART 2 of 3}} a previously unidentified droid society to which [[Vuffi Raa]] from [[L. Neil Smith]]'s [[1983]] trilogy [[Star Wars: The Lando Calrissian Adventures|''The Lando Calrissian Adventures'']] belongs.{{Blog|danwallace|52|Endnotes for The New Essential Guide to Droids: PART 1 OF 3}} How the Heep first came to form an alliance with Screed remained unstated until the [[2013]] release of the second part of the [[Star Wars Blog]] "[[The Droids Re-Animated]]" by [[Rich Handley]] and [[Abel G. Peña]]. In the [[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded|second edition]] of ''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe]]'' ([[1994]]), [[Bill Slavicsek]] described the Great Heep's use of Human slaves to shovel fuel into his boilers. Although this was never shown in "The Great Heep," in ''The New Essential Guide for Droids'' Wallace described the use of such organic slaves as common among Abominor. The second edition of ''A Guide to the Star Wars Universe'' also described the Heep's drive pistons as visible on one side of his body and stated that one of his arms was chopped off at the elbow. These descriptions were repeated in the [[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded|third edition]] ([[2000]]), the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ([[1998]]) and ''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' (1998).''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' However, ''The Great Heep'' clearly shows the Heep with pistons on both sides and with both arms attached. When one arm is severed by lightning at the end of the special, it is at the shoulder, not the elbow. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Great Heep}} *{{DroidsCite|The Great Heep}} {{1st}}
